<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Sam : obj

axiom Close : obj → Prop
axiom StartCooking : obj → Prop
axiom GoShopping : obj → Prop
axiom MakeDinner : obj → Prop
axiom FridgeEmpty : obj → Prop
axiom PantryEmpty : obj → Prop

-- If it is close to dinnertime, then they should start cooking.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Close x → StartCooking x
-- If they go shopping, then they can make dinner.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, GoShopping x → MakeDinner x
-- Either it is close to dinnertime, or they won't make dinner.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Close x ∨ ¬ MakeDinner x
-- If the fridge is empty, then they must go shopping.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, FridgeEmpty x → GoShopping x
-- If there are no ingredients in the pantry, then the fridge is empty.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, PantryEmpty x → FridgeEmpty x

-- From "won't make dinner" we can derive "not pantry empty"
theorem not_make_implies_not_pantry : ∀ x : obj, ¬ MakeDinner x → ¬ PantryEmpty x := by
  intro x
  intro hNotMake
  intro hPantryEmpty
  have hFridgeEmpty : FridgeEmpty x := A5 x hPantryEmpty
  have hGoShopping : GoShopping x := A4 x hFridgeEmpty
  have hMake : MakeDinner x := A2 x hGoShopping
  exact hNotMake hMake

-- A derived disjunction for Sam: close to dinnertime or pantry is not empty
theorem close_or_not_pantry_Sam : Close Sam ∨ ¬ PantryEmpty Sam := by
  have h := A3 Sam
  cases h with
  | inl hClose =>
      exact Or.inl hClose
  | inr hNotMake =>
      have hNotPantry : ¬ PantryEmpty Sam := not_make_implies_not_pantry Sam hNotMake
      exact Or.inr hNotPantry

-- We cannot derive PantryEmpty Sam from StartCooking Sam with the given axioms.
-- In particular, StartCooking Sam does not imply Close Sam, and the available
-- implications flow from PantryEmpty toward MakeDinner, not the reverse.
-- So we cannot prove: StartCooking Sam → PantryEmpty Sam
-- nor can we prove its negation from the given information.
</lean>

ANSWER: Uncertain

  have f : False := hnn n
  exact False.elim f

axiom Hungry : Prop        -- John's coworkers are hungry
axiom Prepared : Prop

axiom Diligent : Prop      -- John is diligent

axiom lunch_implies_hungry : CloseToLunch → Hungry

axiom prepared_implies_ready : Prepared → Ready

-- They were certain that either it was close to lunchtime, or they were not ready for the meeting, or both
axiom certainty : CloseToLunch ∨ ¬Ready ∨ (CloseToLunch ∧ ¬Ready)

theorem not_diligent_if_coworkers_not_hungry : ¬Hungry → ¬Diligent := by
  intro notHungry
  -- From ¬Hungry and (CloseToLunch → Hungry), derive ¬CloseToLunch
  have notLunch : ¬CloseToLunch := by
    intro lunch
    have hHungry : Hungry := lunch_implies_hungry lunch
    exact notHungry hHungry
  -- From the disjunction and ¬CloseToLunch, derive ¬Ready
  have notReady : ¬Ready := by
    cases certainty with
    | inl lunch =>
      exact fun _ => False.elim (notLunch lunch)
    | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl nready => exact nready
      | inr both =>
        exact fun _ => False.elim (notLunch both.left)
  -- From Diligent → Prepared and Prepared → Ready, conclude Diligent → Ready,
  -- which contradicts ¬Ready, hence ¬Diligent
  intro d
  have prep : Prepared := diligent_implies_prepared d
  have ready : Ready := prepared_implies_ready prep
  exact notReady ready

-- Cannot prove (¬Hungry → Diligent) or its negation from the given axioms
</lean>

-- Either the car had gas, or they were not taking the scenic route, or both.

  intro hnd
  intro hgas
  exact hnd (A1 hgas)

  intro hnd
  have hng : ¬Gas they := not_gas_of_not_drive hnd
  have hdisj := A3
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hgas =>
      exact False.elim (hng hgas)
  | inr hrest =>
      cases hrest with
      | inl hnotScenic => exact hnotScenic
      | inr hboth => exact hboth.right

  intro hnd
  have hns : ¬Scenic they := not_scenic_of_not_drive hnd
  have hLtoS : Loves they → Scenic they := fun h => (A4 they h).left
  intro hL
  exact hns (hLtoS hL)

-- From the given axioms, there is no link between 'they' and 'Mike',
-- so we cannot prove Loves Mike or its negation from ¬DriveLong they.
-- Therefore, neither the statement nor its negation is derivable here.
</lean>

axiom R1 : Sunny → WantPlayOutside
-- If flu season begins, then many children will get sick.

-- Either it is sunny, or not many kids get the flu, or it was sunny and many kids did not get sick.

-- If children play outside, they will sleep well at night.

theorem sunny_from_flu : Sunny := by
  have ms : ManySick := R2 T0
  cases R3 with
  | inl s => exact s
  | inr rest =>
    cases rest with
    | inl notMs =>
      exact False.elim (notMs ms)
    | inr hs =>
      exact hs.left

-- Thus children will want to play outside.
theorem want_play : WantPlayOutside := R1 sunny_from_flu

-- However, there is no link from "want to play outside" to "play outside",
-- so we cannot conclude SleepWell, nor its negation, from the given axioms.
-- Hence, we cannot prove SleepWell or ¬SleepWell.
</lean>

axiom A1 : WetDriveway → RainedRecently
-- If someone washed their car, then they used water on the driveway.
axiom A2 : WashedCar → UsedWater

axiom A3 : WetDriveway ∨ ¬UsedWater ∨ (WetDriveway ∧ ¬UsedWater)
-- If the hose is uncoiled, it means someone was washing their car.
axiom A4 : HoseUncoiled → WashedCar
-- Either the hose is uncoiled, or the sponge is soapy, or both.
axiom A5 : HoseUncoiled ∨ SpongeSoapy ∨ (HoseUncoiled ∧ SpongeSoapy)

theorem sponge_soapy_of_not_rained : ¬RainedRecently → SpongeSoapy := by
  intro hNotRain
  -- First show that if it didn't rain, then no water was used.
  have hNotWater : ¬UsedWater := by
    intro hWater
    -- From A3 and hWater, derive WetDriveway.
    have hWet : WetDriveway := by
      have disj := A3
      apply Or.elim disj
      · intro hW
        exact hW
      · intro h2
        apply Or.elim h2
        · intro hNotW
          exact False.elim (hNotW hWater)
        · intro hW_and_notW
          exact False.elim (hW_and_notW.right hWater)
    -- Then it must have rained, contradiction.
    have hRain : RainedRecently := A1 hWet
    exact hNotRain hRain
  -- From no water used, get that the hose was not uncoiled.
  have hNotHose : ¬HoseUncoiled := by
    intro hH
    have hWashed : WashedCar := A4 hH
    have hWater : UsedWater := A2 hWashed
    exact hNotWater hWater
  -- Use the disjunction about hose/spongy to conclude the sponge is soapy.
  have disj2 := A5
  apply Or.elim disj2
  · intro hH
    exact False.elim (hNotHose hH)
  · intro h2
    apply Or.elim h2
    · intro hS
      exact hS
    · intro hHS
      exact hHS.right
</lean>

-- If the supermarket has a sale, then people will stock up on groceries.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : Person, Sale → StockUp x
-- If someone makes a shopping list, then they plan what to buy.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : Person, MakesList x → Plan x
-- Either the store has a sale, or people don't plan what to buy, or there's a sale but they don't plan ahead.
axiom D1 : Sale ∨ (∀ x : Person, ¬ Plan x) ∨ (Sale ∧ (∀ x : Person, ¬ Plan x))
-- If payday was this week, then people make a shopping list.
axiom R3 : Payday → ∀ x : Person, MakesList x
-- Either it was payday this week, or prices went up recently, or both.
axiom D2 : Payday ∨ PricesUp ∨ (Payday ∧ PricesUp)

theorem mary_not_stock_implies_prices_up : (¬ StockUp Mary) → PricesUp := by
  intro hNotStock
  have hCases := D2
  cases hCases with
  | inl hPayday =>
      -- From Payday, everyone makes a list, hence plans; use the disjunction to get Sale
      have hMakesList : ∀ x : Person, MakesList x := R3 hPayday
      have hPlan : ∀ x : Person, Plan x := fun x => R2 x (hMakesList x)
      have hSale : Sale := by
        cases D1 with
        | inl s => exact s
        | inr rest =>
          cases rest with
          | inl allNotPlan =>
              have contra : False := by
                have pMary : Plan Mary := hPlan Mary
                have npMary : ¬ Plan Mary := allNotPlan Mary
                exact npMary pMary
              exact False.elim contra
          | inr both => exact both.left
      have hStock : StockUp Mary := R1 Mary hSale
      exact False.elim (hNotStock hStock)
  | inr rest =>
      cases rest with
      | inl hPrices => exact hPrices
      | inr hBoth => exact hBoth.right
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

axiom PracticedOften : obj → Prop
axiom ImprovedTechnique : obj → Prop
axiom StoodOutHighQuality : obj → Prop
axiom TookArtClass : obj → Prop
axiom LearnedNewMethodsMediums : obj → Prop

-- If someone practiced often, their technique improved.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, PracticedOften x → ImprovedTechnique x
-- If someone's technique improved, their artwork stood out as high quality.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ImprovedTechnique x → StoodOutHighQuality x
-- Either Mary practiced often or took an art class (or both).
axiom E1 : PracticedOften Mary ∨ TookArtClass Mary
-- If someone takes an art class, they learn new methods and mediums.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, TookArtClass x → LearnedNewMethodsMediums x
-- Mary's artwork did not stand out as high quality.
axiom Hnot : ¬ StoodOutHighQuality Mary

theorem mary_learned : LearnedNewMethodsMediums Mary :=
by
  -- From ¬(stood out) and (practiced → improved → stood out), deduce ¬practiced
  have h_not_practice : ¬ PracticedOften Mary :=
    fun hp => Hnot (R2 Mary (R1 Mary hp))
  -- From (practiced ∨ took class) and ¬practiced, deduce took class
  have h_class : TookArtClass Mary :=
    Or.elim E1
      (fun hp => False.elim (h_not_practice hp))
      (fun hc => hc)
  -- Therefore, she learned new methods and mediums
  exact R3 Mary h_class

axiom Emma : obj

-- Predicates
axiom SpeaksDaily : obj → Prop
axiom ImprovedFluency : obj → Prop
axiom ImprovedVocabulary : obj → Prop
axiom RealConversations : obj → Prop
axiom ListensPodcasts : obj → Prop
axiom GainsListening : obj → Prop

-- Axioms from the context
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, SpeaksDaily x → (ImprovedFluency x ∧ ImprovedVocabulary x)
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ImprovedFluency x → RealConversations x
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, SpeaksDaily x ∨ ListensPodcasts x
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, ListensPodcasts x → GainsListening x

-- Goal: If Emma cannot have real conversations, then she gains valuable listening comprehension
theorem emma_gains_listening : ¬ RealConversations Emma → GainsListening Emma := by
  intro hNoConv
  -- Show Emma does not speak daily with native speakers
  have hNotSD : ¬ SpeaksDaily Emma := by
    intro hSD
    have hfluvoc := R1 Emma hSD
    have hFlu : ImprovedFluency Emma := And.left hfluvoc
    have hConv : RealConversations Emma := R2 Emma hFlu
    exact hNoConv hConv
  -- From the disjunction, deduce that Emma listens to podcasts
  have hDisj := R3 Emma
  have hListen : ListensPodcasts Emma := by
    cases hDisj with
    | inl hSD => exact False.elim (hNotSD hSD)
    | inr hLP => exact hLP
  -- Thus she gains valuable listening comprehension
  exact R4 Emma hListen
</lean>

-- If the movie gets good reviews, then many people will go see it.

-- Either the movie got good reviews, or not many people went to see it,
-- or it got good reviews but attendance was still low. (not used in the proof)
axiom A3 : Good m ∨ ¬ Many m ∨ (Good m ∧ ¬ Many m)

-- If a big star is in the movie, then it got good reviews.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, BigStar x → Good x

-- Either there is a big star in it, or it is a sequel, or both.
axiom A5 : BigStar m ∨ Sequel m ∨ (BigStar m ∧ Sequel m)

-- If attendance was low (not many people), then it is a sequel.
theorem low_attendance_implies_sequel : (¬ Many m) → Sequel m := by
  intro hLow
  have hBS_many : BigStar m → Many m := by
    intro hBS
    exact A1 m (A4 m hBS)
  have hNotBS : ¬ BigStar m := by
    intro hBS
    exact hLow (hBS_many hBS)
  have h := A5
  cases h with
  | inl hBS =>
      exact False.elim (hNotBS hBS)
  | inr h' =>
      cases h' with
      | inl hSeq => exact hSeq
      | inr hBoth => exact hBoth.right
</lean>

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Sale → BuyMore x
-- If someone goes grocery shopping, then they will get everything on their list.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, GroceryShopping x → GetAllList x
-- Either the store has a sale, or John did not get everything on his list, or there was a sale and he still did not get all his groceries.
axiom A3 : Sale ∨ (¬GetAllList John ∨ (Sale ∧ ¬GetAllList John))
-- If it is the beginning of the month, then people go grocery shopping.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, BeginningMonth → GroceryShopping x
-- Either it is the beginning of the month, or John's favorite cereal is sold out, or both.
axiom A5 : BeginningMonth ∨ (FavoriteCerealSoldOut John ∨ (BeginningMonth ∧ FavoriteCerealSoldOut John))

theorem john_cereal_sold_out_from_not_buy_more :
  ¬BuyMore John → FavoriteCerealSoldOut John := by
  intro hnb
  -- From ¬BuyMore John, deduce ¬Sale
  have hnsale : ¬Sale := by
    intro hs
    exact hnb (A1 John hs)
  -- From the disjunction A3 and ¬Sale, deduce ¬GetAllList John
  have hnotGet : ¬GetAllList John := by

  exact HnotCheer hc

  cases A3 with

axiom obj : Type

axiom MadeFriends : obj → Prop
axiom Enjoyed : obj → Prop
axiom JoinsClub : obj → Prop
axiom Busy : obj → Prop
axiom Outgoing : obj → Prop
axiom Introduced : obj → Prop

-- If someone made friends, then they would enjoy school.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MadeFriends x → Enjoyed x
-- If someone joins a club, then they will be busy.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, JoinsClub x → Busy x
-- Either they made friends, or they are not busy. (taken universally)
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, MadeFriends x ∨ ¬ Busy x
-- If someone is outgoing, then they would join a club.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Outgoing x → JoinsClub x
-- If someone introduced themselves to classmates, then they are outgoing.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Outgoing x

-- Introduced implies Busy (via Outgoing and JoinsClub)
lemma introduced_implies_busy : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Busy x := by
  intro x
  intro hI
  have hOut : Outgoing x := A5 x hI
  have hJoin : JoinsClub x := A4 x hOut
  exact A2 x hJoin

-- From "made friends → enjoyed", we get "¬enjoyed → ¬made friends"
lemma not_enjoy_implies_not_madefriends : ∀ x : obj, ¬Enjoyed x → ¬MadeFriends x := by
  intro x
  intro hne
  intro hmf
  exact hne (A1 x hmf)

-- From ¬enjoyed, we can derive ¬introduced
lemma not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced : ∀ x : obj, ¬Enjoyed x → ¬Introduced x := by
  intro x
  intro hne
  have hnmf : ¬MadeFriends x := not_enjoy_implies_not_madefriends x hne
  have disj : MadeFriends x ∨ ¬Busy x := A3 x
  have hnbusy : ¬Busy x := Or.elim disj (fun hmf => False.elim (hnmf hmf)) (fun hnb => hnb)
  intro hintro
  have hbusy : Busy x := introduced_implies_busy x hintro
  exact hnbusy hbusy

theorem mark_not_intro_of_not_enjoy : ¬Enjoyed Mark → ¬Introduced Mark :=
  not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced Mark

-- We cannot derive either (¬Enjoyed Mark → Introduced Mark) or its negation from the given axioms:
-- - We proved ¬Enjoyed Mark → ¬Introduced Mark, but we do not have ¬Enjoyed Mark itself,
--   so we cannot conclude ¬(¬Enjoyed Mark → Introduced Mark).
-- - And we cannot prove (¬Enjoyed Mark → Introduced Mark) since it contradicts the derived lemma above unless ¬Enjoyed Mark is false.
</lean>

axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Outgoing x → JoinsClub x
-- If someone introduced themselves to classmates, then they are outgoing.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Outgoing x

-- Introduced implies Busy (via Outgoing and JoinsClub)
lemma introduced_implies_busy : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Busy x := by
  intro x
  intro hI
  have hOut : Outgoing x := A5 x hI
  have hJoin : JoinsClub x := A4 x hOut
  exact A2 x hJoin

-- From "made friends → enjoyed", we get "¬enjoyed → ¬made friends"

  intro hmf

  intro x
  intro hne
  have hnmf : ¬MadeFriends x := not_enjoy_implies_not_madefriends x hne
  have disj : MadeFriends x ∨ ¬Busy x := A3 x
  have hnbusy : ¬Busy x := Or.elim disj (fun hmf => False.elim (hnmf hmf)) (fun hnb => hnb)
  intro hintro
  have hbusy : Busy x := introduced_implies_busy x hintro
  exact hnbusy hbusy

  not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced Mark

--   so we cannot conclude ¬(¬Enjoyed Mark → Introduced Mark).

axiom obj : Type
axiom Mark : obj

axiom MadeFriends : obj → Prop
axiom Enjoyed : obj → Prop
axiom JoinsClub : obj → Prop
axiom Busy : obj → Prop
axiom Outgoing : obj → Prop
axiom Introduced : obj → Prop

-- If someone made friends, then they would enjoy school.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, MadeFriends x → Enjoyed x
-- If someone joins a club, then they will be busy.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, JoinsClub x → Busy x
-- Either they made friends, or they are not busy. (taken universally)
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, MadeFriends x ∨ ¬ Busy x
-- If someone is outgoing, then they would join a club.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Outgoing x → JoinsClub x
-- If someone introduced themselves to classmates, then they are outgoing.
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Outgoing x

-- Introduced implies Busy (via Outgoing and JoinsClub)
lemma introduced_implies_busy : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Busy x := by
  intro x hx
  exact A2 x (A4 x (A5 x hx))

-- From the disjunction (MadeFriends ∨ ¬Busy), Busy rules out ¬Busy, so we get MadeFriends.
lemma introduced_implies_madefriends : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → MadeFriends x := by
  intro x hx
  have hb : Busy x := introduced_implies_busy x hx
  have disj : MadeFriends x ∨ ¬ Busy x := A3 x
  exact Or.elim disj (fun hmf => hmf) (fun hnb => False.elim (hnb hb))

-- Thus Introduced implies Enjoyed.
lemma introduced_implies_enjoyed : ∀ x : obj, Introduced x → Enjoyed x := by
  intro x hx
  exact A1 x (introduced_implies_madefriends x hx)

-- Contraposition: ¬Enjoyed → ¬Introduced.
lemma not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced : ∀ x : obj, ¬Enjoyed x → ¬Introduced x := by
  intro x hne hx
  have henj : Enjoyed x := introduced_implies_enjoyed x hx
  exact hne henj

-- Specific to Mark: if Mark did not enjoy, then he did not introduce.
theorem mark_not_intro_of_not_enjoy : ¬Enjoyed Mark → ¬Introduced Mark :=
  not_enjoy_implies_not_introduced Mark
</lean>

axiom Person : Type
axiom Sam : Person

axiom Hungry : Person → Prop
axiom MakesPancakes : Person → Prop
axiom UsesStove : Person → Prop
axiom AteDinner : Person → Prop

axiom StoveOn : Prop
axiom KitchenHot : Prop

axiom stove_on_implies_hot : StoveOn → KitchenHot
axiom pancakes_imply_use : ∀ p : Person, MakesPancakes p → UsesStove p
axiom stove_on_or_not_use_sam : StoveOn ∨ ¬UsesStove Sam
axiom hungry_implies_pancakes : ∀ p : Person, Hungry p → MakesPancakes p
axiom not_dinner_implies_hungry_sam : ¬AteDinner Sam → Hungry Sam

axiom kitchen_not_hot : ¬KitchenHot

theorem sam_ate_dinner : AteDinner Sam := by
  have hNotOn : ¬StoveOn := fun hOn => kitchen_not_hot (stove_on_implies_hot hOn)
  have hNotUseSam : ¬UsesStove Sam := by
    cases stove_on_or_not_use_sam with
    | inl hOn =>
        exact False.elim (hNotOn hOn)
    | inr hNotUse =>
        exact hNotUse
  classical
  by_contra hNotAte
  have hHungry : Hungry Sam := not_dinner_implies_hungry_sam hNotAte
  have hPanc : MakesPancakes Sam := hungry_implies_pancakes Sam hHungry
  have hUse : UsesStove Sam := pancakes_imply_use Sam hPanc
  exact hNotUseSam hUse
</lean>
